title: Research semantic search solution for saved links
description: Determine the best approach for semantic search over saved links using natural language queries. Must be open source, self-hosted, and inexpensive to operate at ~few saves per day, ~few thousand total links. Consider embedding models, vector databases, and integration with existing architecture.
close_reason: Research complete — decided on Bedrock Titan V2 + sqlite-vec. Documented in research/semantic-search.org

title: Cost monitoring and alerts
Index documents service: read title from metadata and store in vector DB
Paywall piercing capability
Full-text search with ChromaDB
close_reason: Superseded by Bedrock + sqlite-vec approach. ChromaDB dropped.

title: Rewrite Search API for three named result sections
description: Update search-documents-service response shape: {query, parsed: {text, tags}, sections: {vector?, title?, tags?}}. top applies per section. Empty sections omitted. Query parsing: # prefixed tokens → tag search, rest → vector + title search. Vector: existing KNN unchanged. Title: LIKE match on normalized title field (lower, strip # + stop words from query). Tags: sorted by count of matching tags; returns nothing if no # tokens in query. Integration test first, then implement. Don't overspecify the test — just verify all three searches actually run.

title: Build proof-of-concept semantic search comparison

close_reason: No PoC comparison needed — decision made based on research. See research/semantic-search.org

title: Add ruff linter and formatter to pre-commit
002 migration: add tags/title schema + data migration
description:
Create scripts/sqlite-documents-db/migrations/002-add-tags-and-titles.py. Schema changes:
- Add document_tags table: (url TEXT, tag TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (url, tag)), index on tag
- Add full_title column to documents (raw title as originally saved)
- Normalize existing title column: lower(), strip # marks and stop words
Data migration: for each existing document with a title, set full_title = old title, compute normalized title, parse #words into document_tags rows (lowercase, # stripped). Requires S3 read/write via framework. Test against local DB copy first, then run against real DB.

notes: Already implemented in initial infrastructure setup

title: Run 002 migration against real S3 DB
description: After deploy succeeds, run 002 migration against the production DB in S3. Verify migration applied cleanly — check document_tags populated, documents.full_title and normalized title look correct.

title: Evaluate vector database options for small-scale deployment
description: Compare ChromaDB (already in architecture), SQLite with vector extensions, FAISS, Qdrant, and other lightweight vector stores. Consider storage size, query performance, persistence, and ease of deployment for ~few thousand vectors.

close_reason: Fixed: on_part_end used form field name ('document') as key instead of filename ('document.html'/'document.txt'), causing store_document() to always return 400. Added current_filename tracking and regression tests.

title: Create ADR for semantic search solution
description: Write an Architecture Decision Record documenting the semantic search research findings, recommended approach, alternatives considered, and rationale. Submit as PR for review.

title: Update Chrome extension for new search UI
description:
Four changes, no tests needed:
1. Random section order: shuffle the three result sections on each search render (still labeled by type).
2. Click attribution: localStorage tracks clicks and impressions per section type. Log accumulated stats to console on popup open (not at click time).
3. Stats reveal stripe: black <output> element below search results. Click to reveal distribution. Re-hides on each new search to avoid passive bias.
4. Ctrl+click: opens result in background tab (chrome.tabs.create active:false), keeps popup open. Click attribution fires regardless of modifier key.

title: 001 migration: create migrations tracking table
description: Create scripts/sqlite-documents-db/migrations/001-add-migrations-table.py. Creates the migrations table and seeds it with records for both 000 and 001 (marking them as already applied). Uses framework.py context managers. Test against a local DB copy first (set SQLITE_DB_PATH), then run against real DB via S3.

Calculate embedding generation and storage costs at target scale

title: AI-enabled search with embeddings
close_reason: Superseded — semantic search being implemented directly (see new implementation issues)

title: Document storage service: accept optional title param and store in metadata
Set up migration framework (framework.py)
description:
Create scripts/sqlite-documents-db/migrations/framework.py with two context managers:
1. db_connection(script_path): downloads DB from S3 (or uses local path via SQLITE_DB_PATH env var), yields sqlite connection, creates .bak backup before any modification (print backup location), saves back to S3 on exit. Git-ignore .bak files.
2. if_not_applied(conn, script_path): reads migrations table, compares to current script filename, skips body if already applied.

title: Add stop words list to index and search lambdas
description: Find a common English stop words list and save it as a data constant in both index-documents-service and search-documents-service. Used for: (1) normalizing titles at index time — strip # marks and stop words to produce the searchable title field, (2) normalizing the text portion of search queries before title substring matching. Both services must use the same list.
